content:
They are not looking for perfect syntax. They are looking for hidden costs, weak ownership, unsafe assumptions, and the places the code will hurt later.

16 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

What Senior Developers Notice in Your Code Before You DoThe first time a senior developer reviewed my code seriously, I thought they were being too picky.
The feature worked.
The page rendered. The API returned the right data. The form was submitted. The loading spinner appeared. The button was disabled when it needed to be disabled. The bug I was assigned to fix was gone.
I expected comments about formatting, naming, maybe a small suggestion about simplifying a condition.
Instead, the comments were about things I had not even considered.
Who owns this state?
What happens if this request finishes after the user changes filters?
Why is this permission rule only checked in the UI?
Why does the export build filter differently from the table?
Can this function mutate the original object?
What happens if this value is null for old records?
